reviews.review_title: Dog left me with no dih 🥀
reviews.review_description:
I put on my Oculus like a tactical genius even though I was standing in my living room in pajama pants with zero survival instincts. My ex K-9 unit dog watched from the corner, eyes half-lidded, clearly thinking, “I survived real combat for this?” I booted up Ghosts of Tabor forgot every control instantly, and fired my first virtual gunshot like a clown discovering firearms.

The sound blasted through the headset and my dog’s ears snapped up with government-issued urgency. I said, “It’s just a game,” which legally counts as summoning doom. Another gunshot rang out, and he rose slowly, dramatically, like a war movie veteran realizing the past had come back and it was wearing VR goggles. In his mind, I was no longer his owner—I was an armed suspect in pajama pants.

Before I could react, he launched. In VR, I spun in circles firing wildly; in real life, I was windmilling my arms and screaming at furniture. He slammed into me, the headset slid sideways, and my vision became a cursed split-screen of couch cushions and digital death. He went straight for my dih shredding my pants and my dih with the precision of a trained professional, while I hit a pitch usually reserved for car alarms.

Then, just like that, he disengaged, sat down perfectly, and stared at me like, “Threat neutralized. Maybe don’t start a firefight indoors.” The game announced I’d been eliminated, which felt rude considering I’d already lost the battle, the round, and my dignity. He slept like a hero that night while I questioned why technology keeps finding creative new ways to humble me.

Ghosts of Tabor 2/5 star
Extremely immersive. Activated a retired K-9, destroyed my pants, taught me nothing. Would not recommend playing near anyone with combat training or common sense.

The Ghost of Tabor is often remembered as a mysterious and eerie place, but when you look deeper, it can also be seen as something negative rather than fascinating. While ghost towns may seem interesting because of their history, Tabor represents failure, abandonment, and the consequences of poor planning. Instead of being a symbol of adventure, it shows how quickly a community can fall apart and be left behind.

One reason the Ghost of Tabor can be considered “bad” is because it reflects economic collapse. Towns like Tabor were often built quickly with high hopes, usually around farming or trade, but they didn’t last. When resources ran out or people moved away, everything was left to decay. This kind of history highlights instability and poor long-term planning. Rather than being a success story, Tabor reminds us of how communities can fail when they rely too much on uncertain opportunities.

Another negative aspect is the sense of abandonment and loss tied to ghost towns. Homes, buildings, and streets that were once full of life are now empty. This creates a feeling of isolation and even danger. For some people, these places can be unsettling rather than exciting. They also show how people were forced to leave their homes behind, which can represent hardship and struggle rather than something positive or inspiring.

Finally, the Ghost of Tabor can be seen as “bad” because it contributes to misconceptions about history. Some people romanticize ghost towns as cool or mysterious, but that can ignore the real struggles that caused them to be abandoned. Instead of focusing on lessons learned, people sometimes treat them like entertainment. This can take away from understanding the real challenges faced by the people who once lived there.

In conclusion, while the Ghost of Tabor might seem interesting at first, it represents more negative ideas than positive ones. It reflects economic failure, abandonment, and misunderstood history. Rather than just being a spooky or cool place, Tabor serves as a reminder of how quickly things can go wrong when a community is not built to last.

reviews.review_title: I cant even recommend this game
reviews.review_description:
After many many enjoyed hours playing Tabor, i can not recommend anyone buys the game. 

Simply put, Tabor has been out for 3 years. Game feels janky AF there is multiple $10 DLC and still alot that could be improved in the core experience. 

My honest opinion, buy Contractors Exfil. Absolutely every single aspect of contractors is better except one thing. The draw distance in Tabor is drastically better than Contractors, how ever that is probably because Contractors maps feel like they have 50 times the amount of everything, more loot, more buildings, more enemies. 

There is nothing about Tabor that can make me reccomend the game at this point. Simply put there is a far better more engaging option for basically the same price. 

I loved Tabor for many hours, alot of fun times but once i played Contractors it felt like i was eating bread at home with Tabor while contractors took me to a steak house. 

Tabor could most likely destroy Contractors if they stopped pumping out $10 DLC and focused on the actual game, add more to maps, make AI like 1000000x better than they are now because the AI is a joke. You need to redo all the gun sounds again, after the MASSIVE sound update the guns still sounded like crap. Literally PS2 quality not trying to be rude this is just my honest opinion after countless hours in both games. 

There is absolutely nothing about Tabor that makes it worth the purchase if your looking for the most out of a VR extraction shooter that is my opinion. If you enjoy Tabor more than Contractors than im happy for you and glad you can enjoy what you want, i just couldnt continue playing the worse option from my perspective.

Ghosts of Tabor was once a great & unique game. The only actual VR extraction shooter like EFT.
After a while, the game got bigger and stopped having to stand out, it experienced a problem that many studios do when going from indie to big. They began to update the game with smaller and easier changes like useless bug fixes and more DLCs and collabs. The biggest updates have been controversial and mostly hated like the Island and Bunker rework.

I don't want this review to be all negative so I'll point out some things that keep me playing rather than their competitor.
1. Style: The game is dark and gritty, its more 'realistic' and immersive.
2. Originality: Things like Yorick's skull, the USB stick and the upcoming hacking device all having unique functions that can be used in raid are very cool.
3. Legacy: It's the original VR Extraction Shooter.

Some things are good but can 100% be improved.
1. PvE mode. I personally use PvE mode due to lack of time, motivation and consistency in lifestyle which are all often vital to playing extraction shooters, needing to keep up with everyone else.
PvE mode, to put it bluntly, isn't very good. There's no real challenge, bad customization and bad progression. Due to the lack of PMCs, who are a vital part of progression, the game's progression is slowed down and too easy at the same time since you can't get gear from killing others, the most you'll get without a boss is a chest rig most people can already buy. The customization only changes health and damage values rather than making the AI more challenging and there's no real reason to use harder difficulties due to loot levels not even scaling.

I can't type much more due to text restrictions but here is a longer version that will be being updated: slopificationoftabor (ends in  dot straw dot page)

If you are interested in buying:

This game is great. The community is a little bit goofy sometimes, beware of that. If you like tarkov, arc raiders or marathon, you will definitely like this too. Don't let the overwhelming challenge overtake your thoughts, as progression is great in this game if you know what you are doing. Great game. Definitely could improve.

For yall crybaby:

First of all, labs controlled old island. 'yeah but orange keycard helped' no, since you'd still have to pass through labs.
Second of all, that's the nostalgia and experience, not the map.
Third of all, yes, the new map looks duller and lower saturated than old island, and i hate that myself, but the expansion of it is great.

The dlc's are a way for extra profit of the dev team and buying them is completely optional. They are cheap too, can't y'all spare a buck or sum??

Yes, the game is buggy. No, they aren't permanent. Instead of crying about it, go to the discord server and bug report it.

I really enjoy this game as well as many others while yes just like any other game it has its share of bugs and glitches, it is overall a really fun and enjoyable game. I 100% recommend this as the best VR extraction shooter that I’ve played and at the time I’m making this I think the only two extraction shooters are ghosts of tabor and contractors exfil zone.

Do you want to spend two days trying to get the boss to spawn? Then this game is for you. 

Do you want to finally kill the boss after he spawns then not be able to take his vest because it falls through the ground? Then this game is for you. 

Do you want to take green card to underground and end up with a million dollar Ragnar only to have it completely disappear from your hand when you go to eat? Then this game is for you. 

Do you want to hit purple card and fill up a Ragnar with five of the best armors in the game only to have the same thing happen when you go to put the bag on your back? Then this game is for you. 

Do you want half the guns on your wall to just vanish and never return? Then this game is for you. 

Do you want half your missions to be permanently bugged so you can't progress? Then this game is for you.

If you want a game with more bugs than the Florida Everglades then play this game. 
If not then go play Exfil Zone. 

This game's inability to be played certainly comes down to "skill issue" specifically the developers having a skill issue when it comes to making a functioning game.

reviews.review_title: Was good, got better, hit rock bottom
reviews.review_description: I’ve played this game for probably hundreds of hours and have thoroughly enjoyed it. It was a great game to play with friends and although it had its buffers here and there it was great. Until the new revamp…After that the game has hit rock bottom in my opinion in gameplay, graphics, the whole nine yards. You load into a match after running around your bunker for 2 minutes just trying to find where your gear is and whilst in the process you passed 3 pointless rooms that are just decoys basically. Once you get in you spend about 30 seconds walking until you meet an NPC that can somehow shoot you through walls and takes an ENTIRE MAG TO KILL. You are now on less than 50% health. You eventually find yourself outnumbered and out-kitted, but you try to fight the people for the thrill and the 0.0000000001% chance you actually get their loot. Only for all your hopes and dreamss to be slammed down a garbage chute the second you peak out from cover because somehow they snap to your head and just take it off. Or the other option which I didn’t mention is to get killed by a corner camping Timmy. Btw Even when you think you could win a gunfight , your gun will randomly grab on to harry potters magic broom and just fly to your left shoulder where you can no longer aim and shoot for accuracy. 4/10 as of now, would not recommend unless you want your time to be wasted within a span of 5 minutes and be pissed off for the rest of the day.

What a shame. This game had everything to be sensational; it's extremely challenging, progress is difficult, requiring patience, practice, and strategy... however, it has a poor and lazy development team that prefers to blame players for "skill issues" rather than truly investing in improvements. It's full of bugs and crashes. The game apparently malfunctions in complex situations that require a lot of processing power, such as firefights or when dealing with many items nearby—in other words, at the moments when you need it most. With such difficult progress, the bugs make things almost unsustainable. Things get even worse for South American players, who face a disadvantage of more than 1 second in combat that is sometimes decided with just one shot because there's no local server. Unfortunately, after many hours of playing, my patience ran out, and I switched to Exfil.

Everything written here reflects my genuine opinion and experiences with the game; AI was only used to help organize and formalize my thoughts into writing.
Ghosts of Tabor used to be one of the best gaming experiences I’ve ever had before all of the major updates and DLCs started changing the game. Some of my favorite memories from childhood came from playing with my brothers — battling through raids, talking strategy after victories, and just having an incredible time together.
Back then, the game felt simple in the best way possible. Every match felt exciting, tense, and full of real players. Now it feels like too much is being added too quickly. The maps often feel empty of actual players while being overcrowded with Fenix enemies, and a lot of the charm that made the game special feels lost.
It’s sad to see a game that meant so much to me start following the path of bigger corporations, focusing more on adding content and monetization instead of preserving what made the experience great in the first place. Ghosts of Tabor will always be a huge part of my childhood, and I’ll always appreciate the memories it gave me. I still wish the game could return to the way it once was, even though deep down I know it probably never will. And also, all those 5 star reviews you see? From years ago, when the game was its peak. And Scott, if you read this… bring it back, please…

Well I recently got my first quest, which I’m kind of behind on the trend since I’m pretty much an unc now. But this game is rather.. interesting. I load in to my first couple raids and get smoked harder than a hotbox by some ai with an sks. Then I go to chud mall, more known as goober city. And decide to see what pvp is like there. As I make my way into chud city or what people like to call the mall, two Tatra kits rush me with LMGs or whatever, and the most loot I’ve ever seen. Instead of shooting me they make me come out with my beginner kit I got from the rations. They then proceed to call me several slurs and do… sexual things with me. I then decide to run away because I personally don’t like getting touched, which then follows with me getting smoked and sent to a black screen faster than JF Kennedy. No offense by what I’m saying, this game is one interesting s*it show, but still, W game ig. 🤞

reviews.review_title: Ghost of tabor is nice but?
reviews.review_description: A very fun game honestly but it feels like the updates aren't fixing much about the graphics are nice but It looks like it's made to be heavily made for better performance and the gun play feels hard not in a good way the health system needs a rework so the health feels more reasonable like close to every other extraction shooter the pain system is literally non-existent it only does suck a small thing for such a small time that it doesn't do anything also I like the stims but it's pretty buns the game unfortunately still feels like it's in Early Access and I'm sad because I'm not just saying it's buns because I play contractors I have the 2 wipe award please please just put up a few polls asking hey new gun sounds? Or hey should we remake the health system/ttk speed or are you fine with what it's looking like now or hey are you guys should we make the pain system better or a rework? Or should we add a graphics selection system? Or do you think it's fine how it is now.

It has been my favorite game for years. Many bugs, but every one is workable and there are constant hotfixes that improve and help the game. Yes, the devs are slow sometimes, but they do get around to things. The story is also very good. Even though there is almost no clues, the traders and the maps give you it. Anyways, it’s a very fun game. Hard. Random. But good. You’ll die with a tatra kit to a naked timmy, but at the same time, you might go in as a Timmy and leave with Tatra armor. All a matter of patience. 

Trust no one.

This game is amazing it is made for every type of person( the ones who like a challenge and pvp, the people who like pave and like to chill) I love the performance of everything, it is relatively realistic(especially if you get a gunstock) there are a few bugs currently, but they are working on it. Great game highly recommend! God bless you!
